steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The line of best fit must match the scatterplot's slope and $y$-intercept. The data drops roughly 70 units over 7 units of $x$, giving a slope near $-10$. A slope of $-0.1$ would change $y$ by less than 1 unit total — far too shallow. This eliminates B and C.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 80 - 10x$: at $x = 3$, predicts $80 - 30 = 50$, but the point $(3, 60)$ is visible on the plot. ✗
  • Choice B — slope $-0.1$: slope is far too small for the steep decline shown. ✗
  • Choice C — slope $-0.1$: same issue. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 90 - 10x$: at $x = 3$, predicts $90 - 30 = 60$. Matches $(3, 60)$ exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
